reason: Low organic engagement, fake account crisis, and a data-exposure security breach accelerated the shutdown
lesson: Forcing users onto a new social network by bundling it with existing products creates accounts, not engagement

reason: Overpriced, carrier-exclusive (AT&T only), and the gimmick 3D features added no real utility
lesson: Hardware differentiation must solve a real problem — novelty alone cannot justify a premium price or a locked ecosystem

reason: Mobile-only format prevented casting to TV, launched during COVID when commutes vanished, and content failed to build loyal audiences
lesson: Validate your core use-case assumption (on-the-go viewing) before raising $1.8B and launching

reason: Low creator adoption, vertical-only format frustrated creators, and TikTok dominated short-form video attention
lesson: Short-form video beats long-form on mobile — meet users where their attention already is

reason: Arrived years after the iPod had already won the portable music market; the ecosystem never attracted enough content
lesson: Entering a winner-take-all market years late requires a fundamentally different value proposition, not a feature-parity clone

reason: Snapchat had already captured the ephemeral messaging behavior; Poke felt like a derivative copy with no unique hook
lesson: Cloning a competitor's format into your existing product rarely works when the original already owns the behavior

reason: Clubhouse's core DAU had already collapsed before payments could reach scale; too few active creators remained
lesson: Monetisation features only work if the underlying engagement is healthy — do not build creator revenue tools while retention is in freefall

reason: Users who wanted ephemeral Stories already used Instagram or Snapchat; Fleets added friction to Twitter's text-first experience
lesson: Importing a feature format that works elsewhere does not guarantee it fits the interaction model of a different product

reason: Premium scripted content failed to compete with free UGC and larger streaming budgets; Snapchat's audience expected authentic, not polished
lesson: Platform-native content expectations are hard to override — users come for authentic peer content, not broadcast-quality productions

reason: High latency on average home internet, a thin game library, full-price purchases with no ownership guarantee, and strong competition from Xbox Cloud Gaming
lesson: Cloud gaming requires near-perfect infrastructure before launch; charging full price for games on an unproven platform destroys trust immediately
